official_title: Courts Rules of Court Order No. 1 of 2017 in Respect of Legal Representation in Hearings Before the Small Claims Tribunal of the DIFC Courts JANUARY 16, 2017 COURT ADMINISTRATIVE ORDERS of Order No. 1DIFC Courts (/glossary/DIFC-courts/) Rules (/glossary/rules/) Court (/glossary/court/) of 2017 in Respect of Legal Representation in Hearings Before the Small Claims of the DIFC Courts Tribunal (/glossary/tribunal/) I, Michael Hwang, of the DIFC Courts, make the followingChief Justice (/glossary/chief-justice/) Order: In exercise of the powers conferred on me by Article 8(3)(a) of Dubai Law No. 9 of 2004, as amended; and after having reviewed: Dubai Law No. 9 of 2004 in respect of the , as amended; Dubai Law No. 12 of DIFC (/glossary/DIFC/) 2004 in respect of the Judicial Authority at the DIFC, as amended; DIFC Order No. 1 of 2014 in respect of the Rules of the DIFC Courts 1. This Order may be cited as the Rules of Court Order No. 1 of 2017 2. The Rules of the DIFC Courts (RDC) Part 53.52 and 53.53 shall be amended to read as follows: “Representation at a hearing 53.52 Natural persons (1) Unless the SCT orders otherwise, a party should present his own case atJudge (/glossary/judge/) a hearing (2) A party may be represented at the hearing by a non-lawyer or lawyer[1] only after obtaining permission from the SCT which is to be given where it appears to the SCT on reasonable grounds that it is necessary in the circumstances
